questions.question: Why can perceptual audio coding discard bits without audible loss?
options: It uses a smaller sample rate
It removes components below the psychoacoustic masking threshold
questions.explanation: The masking threshold tells the encoder which frequency components the ear cannot hear, so they can be quantized coarsely or dropped.

questions.question: What does the signal-to-mask ratio (SMR) determine in a perceptual encoder?
options: How many bits are allocated to each subband
questions.explanation: Subbands with high SMR (signal well above the masking threshold) get more bits; subbands with low or negative SMR get few or zero bits.

questions.question: Why does AAC achieve the same perceived quality as MP3 at a lower bitrate?
options: AAC uses a larger sample size
AAC replaces the hybrid filter bank with a pure MDCT and adds tools like TNS
questions.explanation: AAC uses a pure MDCT filter bank with better frequency resolution, improved Huffman codebooks, and tools such as Temporal Noise Shaping, making it more efficient than MP3.

questions.question: What is simultaneous masking?
options: A loud tone makes nearby quiet tones inaudible at the same time
questions.explanation: Simultaneous masking means a strong signal at one frequency raises the detection threshold for nearby frequencies, so quieter components there are not heard.
